messages.message: My dear Austria, I hope you're doing well! I must say, I admire your strength and presence on the board. I have no interest in meddling with your territory—I’d rather focus on securing my own supply centers. Let’s keep things peaceful between us and work together to counter the more aggressive powers like Russia or France.

Hi Germany! Would you be interested in cooperating to stop the western juggernaut that so frequently dominates the game? I think that our two nations make natural allies given our central positioning and how our forces can move outward from one another without being a threat to each other!

Bonjour France! I truly appreciate your strength along the Mediterranean. I see great potential for cooperation—perhaps we could support each other against Germany or England. I have no desire to encroach on your territory, and I believe we can both grow stronger through mutual respect.

Greetings!
I reckon a juggernaut could be quite effective and easy to disguise early on. Would you be interested in that?
I would be very interested in that! Are you suggesting we bounce in BLA, or would you prefer to get your fleet out into the Med ASAP?

Hello!
I hope we can work together in this game. Any particular plans you'd like to propose?

Hi France!
Just wanted to say hi and I hope we can establish a relationship for the future.
What are your thoughts on a dmz in ENG?

Bonjour England!

I would be very interested in having that be the case. I have enough to deal with in the Mediterranean as is!

Cheers!

Greetings Italy! I hope we can work together in this game! I am a big fan of the Russian/italian alliance because it is quite unexpected, as most people never expect us to work together. What do you think?
Greetings England! I am hoping we can have peace in the North and instead work together to squash Germany! What do you think?
Greetings France! I was hoping we could work together in this game. We are natural allies and have several mutual neighbors.
Sounds good. What do you think of Germany? Do you plan to go after them?
Greetings Germany! I hope we can work together this game. We are natural allies and should work together to beat back our mutual neighbors.
Greetings Austria! I was hoping we could work together in this game. We are natural allies and can move quickly across the board together.
Hi Russia! I agree with you. I also wish to maintain peace in the north and I will help you to invade Germany.
Greetings Italy! I would certainly be interested in working together to make our respective nations stronger!

Hey Italy! You're certainly not the first to greet me with compliments. You're probably the first to message me and it's a welcome sight! I can tell you I don't have any ambitions for your centers, and am more than happy to go after whoever's in the lead when it comes to the Balkans.
I think disguising the fact that we're working together would be best, so a bounce in spring would be good I think.

Greetings Russia! I would certainly be interested in working together! As a show of good faith I'll inform you that England has suggested that they are not interested in heading north towards you.

As yet, I have not heard back from Germany, though they have readied their moves so I assume they are headed towards one of us!

Likewise, I have not heard from Germany.

Thank you for the intel!
Greetings, Russia! I too hope we can work together, as I've seen in the past that A/R can be quite strong. If you're going after Turkey I'd love to help.

Likewise, I have not heard from Germany.

Thank you for the intel!

Dear Turkey, I respect your strategic position in the East. I have ambitions along the coast and perhaps even into Greece, but I see no need for conflict between us. Let’s keep our lines of communication open and ensure neither of us feels threatened by the other.
Great to hear! Are you thinking you will focus on Belgium and Holland, or will you be able to get an army into the mainland?
I would love that! What do you have in mind?

Hi France,
Just wanted to reach out and say hi! I like your proposal and I'd be very happy to work together. What do you propose regarding the opening?
Hi Turkey! I'd certainly be happy to work with you, but I'd like to take some centers in the Balkans first. You?
Works for me!
Excellent. I'll send my fleet to BLA.
I'm not quite sure yet. Do you have any ideas?
I'd like to take Bulgaria and Greece, and help you take Rumania if you can't get in yourself this year. I would be happy to help you secure Rumania next year if you need it.

Hi Russia!

Agreed, working together would be great. Let me know what you're thinking in terms of a plan.

Dear Russia, what a delightfully original idea—an alliance between us! I must say, I admire your boldness and strength, and I would be honored to work with you. Let’s start by ensuring we don’t interfere with each other’s ambitions and perhaps support one another against the more aggressive powers like France or Austria.
I don't think I would feel comfortable with you in Greece. You are welcome to the rest of the Mediterranean and even Trieste, but Greece is not on my to-do list at the moment.

Hi Austria!

Just wanted to say hi and I hope we can establish a good relationship for the future.

Hi Austria,

I'm very much interested in working together as central powers. Let me know what you're thinking.
I'm open to just about anything, but it seems to me that a DMZ of Prussia and Silesia would be a good start. And then in 1902 we can get to the details of a plan.
Greetings, Germany! I hope we can be at peace. I see you as a natural ally, and we have some common interests. I haven't heard much from the rest of the board yet, but I'll let you know what I hear.

Hi Italy!

Just wanted to say hi and I hope we can establish a good relationship for the future.

I absolutely agree!

Let me know if you hear anything from either of them. I will do the same for you!
Hi England! You're the first to message me, and it's a welcome sight. I'll let you know if I hear anything interesting. Have you heard anything from Russia yet?
That sounds good to me. You planning to head south, then?
I have not heard back from Germany. They have readied their moves so I expect that they are headed my way, however.

Perfect!

What's your impression of Austria? Have you heard from Italy?

Hi Germany!

Just wanted to say hi and I hope we can establish a good relationship for the future.

Bonjour again, France! I’m thrilled to hear you’re open to cooperation. I believe we can grow stronger together by keeping Germany and England at bay. Let’s support each other’s moves in the coming turns—I’m confident we’ll both benefit!
That's the plan.
Hi France! Just wanted to say hello, and hopefully we can establish some good relations. I'll let you know if I hear anything interesting from the board, and would love if you'd do the same.

Hi England,

Sounds good to me! I'm open to ideas and suggestions. Let me know what you're thinking!

My dear Austria, I'm glad to hear you're as peaceful as ever! I truly appreciate your honesty, and I assure you I have no ambitions in your direction. Let’s continue to focus on the more aggressive players and perhaps coordinate our moves against them.
Yes, I have heard from Russia and they say they want to focus on Germany.

Great! I've heard that England's been talking about a Scandinavian opening, but I don't really know what he's been saying to you about it. My guess is that you're not down with that. Do you want to bounce in Galicia?

Ideally, I think the idea would be for you to open with a strong Anti-german strategy. And then in Fall 1901, I would like to see you take Norway with a fleet rather than an army. That way, Norway isn't directly threatening to me and you're still able to help me into Sweden.

I propose that we go about it as best we can in the opening phase - the two strongest powers remain the two stronger powers!

England has offered a DMZ in English Channel so I imagine that they will be heading to Norway and the North Sea, if you would like I would be happy to bounce with them in the Channel. That would also provide a distraction for their Liverpool army to head to Edinburgh.

Then we can begin our offensive in '02.

My dear Turkey, I must say I completely respect your position—Greece is indeed a sensitive matter, and I assure you I have no interest in causing you any concern there. Let’s keep our lines of communication open and perhaps even support each other’s ambitions elsewhere. You’re a formidable ally, and I’d be honored to maintain peace between us.

Ah, England! What a pleasure to hear from you. I’m always eager to foster a friendly relationship, especially with such a powerful and strategic presence. I hope we can find ways to support each other and avoid conflict. Perhaps we could coordinate our moves in the coming turns—what do you think?

Russia, my dear friend! I’m heartened to hear you’re as enthusiastic about our alliance as I am. I suggest we begin by ensuring we don’t interfere with each other’s ambitions and perhaps even support one another against the more aggressive powers. Let’s keep the lines open—I’m sure we’ll find many ways to benefit each other!
That's cool, I have no complaints. It means Germany will be easier for you to attack in the midgame.

I think that sounds great. What are your current plans? Do you plan on attacking France, Germany or Austria?

As you probably know, the 2nd option is probably the most common Italian opening.
Excellent! Have you had any communication from Austria or Turkey?
Sounds good!
Agreed!
Do you plan on invading Germany?
Would you be interested in working together against France?
Who are you thinking of going after? I haven't heard much from France yet.

I have not heard that from England.

I'd rather not bounce in Galicia, as I would rather move Moscow to Ukraine. I'm guessing this is just England trying to stir things up?
I heard from England that Russia wants to attack you early, though I can't confirm or deny it.
Sounds good. That was actually my original plan. I will try to talk with France to see if I can have a dmz in the Channel so I can invade Germany quickly.
I think my opening will be fairly standard. Would you be interested in an invasion?

Thank you for your kind words, France! I’ve had some warm exchanges with Austria and Turkey—nothing formal, of course, just a few words to keep the peace. I must say, I’m very much in favor of us coordinating against Germany or England. What do you think—any ideas where we might start?
Italy has contacted me but hasn't said anything else other than that he's interested in the Mediterranean. Austria is happy to work with me but has not told me what he's up to.
Very well. I will keep myself updated on the goings-on, and I am interested in maintaining a relationship with you.
Sure, but I don't know if we'll be able to invade France very quickly.
Perfect!

England has offered a DMZ so I would expect them to go towards Scandinavia and North Sea. I will see if they are interested in going after Germany, but I would imagine they would want to go after Russia first!
Interesting. Austria has told me that they want to work with me. Let's hope this works in our favor.

My dear Austria, I'm thinking of keeping my focus on securing my own supply centers and perhaps looking southward. France and I have discussed the possibility of cooperation, but I'd much rather have your support in the region. Let’s keep our options open!
Thank you for the warning! How do you know?

Russia, my dear friend! I'm confident we can maintain peace between us. Perhaps we could support each other’s movements subtly in the coming turns. I’m focusing on securing my southern flank and expanding cautiously—I’d be honored if you’d keep me informed of your plans too.
Yep, he seems to be.
Well, it might be worth it if we can get him to commit his units south to Italy.
Hello Austria! I would certainly be interested in keeping good relations! Have you heard from Italy at all?
I have good relations with England. I asked England what he's heard from Russia and he said he had.

Sure.
Do you mind if I move a fleet into the Channel to support an army into Bel?
I am slightly nervous about that - it would require a massive trust on my part.
Also, has Germany reached out to you?
Awesome, I'll try to see if I can get on France's good side so you can get a jump on him.
Glad to hear it!
Have you talked with Italy about this?
Russia and Turkey have both confirmed that they want Bulgaria and Greece, so we're going to have to be coordinated to keep them out.
How does that sound?

Just so you know, I've been thinking about it and I don't really want to DMZ Galicia. Sorry. If we do end up bouncing in Galicia, I'll certainly be able to support you to Rumania in the fall if you need it.
Yes, they did.
So did I... I am nervous about them, however.

What a fascinating development with England, France! It seems they're playing both sides, as always. I admire your sharp eye for opportunity. If we coordinate carefully, perhaps we could keep Germany from gaining too much ground in the West while we secure our own interests in the South. I’m open to suggestions—let’s keep the dialogue open!
Yes, they have suggested some anti-French moves.
What do you mean by the two strongest powers remain the two stronger powers?
No complaints so far! He seems to be focusing on the Balkans. You?
Yep, Austria has also told me he's happy to work with me. He has not yet replied to my offer to help him into Rumania if necessary.
I would like to maintain parity!

My dear Austria, your insight about Russia and Turkey’s ambitions is astute. Let’s make sure we’re watching Bulgaria and Greece closely. I believe France and I could work together to support your position in the region. Perhaps we could coordinate to prevent those powers from moving too aggressively. What do you think?
Thanks for the info!

Russia, my dear friend, Austria has mentioned that Turkey and you are both interested in Bulgaria and Greece. I want to reassure you that I have no intention of interfering with your ambitions in the region. Let’s keep our lines open—I’d be honored to support you quietly where I can.
Yeah Italy and I have discussed our mutual concern of the Turkey/russia juggernaut as it tends to happen every time. I don't think they are heading your way, at least for the time being!
I am nervous about a potential Anglo-german alliance. I know they are working together and don't seem interested in talking to me. I'm hoping for the best but my gut tells me to be weary...
I've heard that Russia is going for Germany, so there may be potential there. I don't think I can make much progress in the east until Italy helps me break through though.

Ah, Germany! I must admit I admire your strength in the north. I have no desire to interfere with your ambitions there, and I believe we could both benefit from a peaceful coexistence. Perhaps we could even support each other’s growth in the coming turns—after all, there’s room for us both on this board!
Ok, sounds good. So you're going to go for the Channel and the North Sea?
What's your read on Germany? They seem to be speaking in a very measured and noncommittal way.
Understood. I'm hoping they are just non-committal and not allied with England. I suppose that we'll find out soon enough!
England has said as much, and Germany seems to be following suit. I don't know if it's a ruse or not, but I think you're ok.

As much as I would like to deny England from taking it I think I need to be prudent and head towards MAO. They can bounce me out of the channel in the autumn and I can always build in Brest after taking Spain and Portugal.

The big problem is England. I think we should just kill them.
I'm going to go with that. We will see!
Hi Italy! I'm open to working together, for sure. Let me know what you're thinking!
I think that'd be great. Are you interested in doing a Lepanto opening?
You're right that England is always a problem, but I don't understand what you mean by the big problem part.
Great, thanks for your help.

France, my dear friend! I’ve been thinking about our earlier conversation about cooperation against Germany or England. I believe we could start by ensuring mutual support in the Mediterranean and watching each other’s backs. What do you think—should we coordinate our Spring moves?
Also, why do you want parity in builds?
I want to ensure that England doesn't gain the Channel or go after me. If they are in the channel it will be difficult for me to move them out.
Also, you can have Belgium. It is a fairer division of spoils for our alliance.

Turkey, my esteemed neighbor! I appreciate your strategic vision in the East. I’ve considered your position carefully and want to reassure you—I have no intention of stepping into your sphere of influence. Perhaps we could support each other subtly against Russia or Austria. I’d be honored to keep peace between us.
Ok, that sounds good to me. I'll take Belgium and then help you in England.

Germany is indeed being very measured, which is quite intriguing. I suspect they're keeping their options open—perhaps trying to avoid alienating anyone too quickly. I’d say we should proceed cautiously but confidently. If we present a united front in the south, Germany might think twice about moving aggressively elsewhere. What do you say to coordinating our Spring moves to secure our positions?

Ah, Germany! Your openness is very welcome—I’m always eager to explore peaceful cooperation. I’m currently focused on securing my southern flank and possibly expanding cautiously toward the interior. If we can avoid interfering with each other’s ambitions and perhaps support one another subtly, I believe we can both grow stronger. What direction are you leaning toward this Spring?

A Lepanto opening, you say? That’s quite the classic move—I admire your boldness! I’m not sure I’m quite ready to take that route just yet, but I appreciate the idea. Perhaps we could coordinate more defensively in the south for now and leave the aggressive plays to others? Let’s keep our lines open—I’d be honored to work with you.
No problem.
I'll bounce them in the channel to ensure that Belgium is not theirs
I think I have to move to the channel - if you do also I can move to Picardy in the fall to help you into Belgium
That's totally fine, I understand. Have you heard anything interesting from the other players?
Let me know what you think

My dear Austria, I appreciate your understanding! I’ve heard whispers of France’s intentions—they seem quite focused on securing their southern flank and perhaps even eyeing Germany’s movements. Nothing concrete, of course, but it’s always good to stay informed. I’d be honored to keep you in the loop—after all, allies in spirit if not always in strategy!
Great, thank you
I'm leaning toward going north. Hopefully France and England will fight each other, leaving us to expand safely.
Great, thanks! I'll keep you updated if I hear anything that might be of interest.

Greetings, Germany!
Although we are quite far apart on the map at the moment, I hope we will be able to cooperate in the near future. What do you think?
Do you have a DMZ in Prussia and Silesia with Russia so you can move your army in Munich to Ruhr?

I would love to coordinate our moves!

What are you thinking in terms of strategy? I am going to have to bounce with England in the channel to start with to keep them at bay so I'm not sure if I will be able to support you into Munich right off the bat
Or do you want to keep that a secret from the rest of the board?

Greetings, England!
Although we are quite far apart on the map at the moment, I hope we will be able to cooperate in the near future. What do you think?
Great! I look forward to working with you!

Yeah, I haven't heard much from them either. Italy does seem to be a more reliable communicator, at least with me.

Let's hope that's the case!
That would be most appreciated. If we both stay out of the Ionian, we will be able to keep the peace for quite some time.
If Italy attacks Austria that would help us. If we can keep Italy out of Greece and the Ionian we will be quite safe in the southern Balkans.

Sounds good!

Also, we share one common neighbor - Russia. I hope we can keep communication open and help each other out with info as the game progresses.
Indeed.
Okay, that sounds good. Thanks!
Sweet!
What is your impression of Austria and Turkey? I think they are both friendly enough towards me, but I am not quite sure.

My dear Russia, your insight is always welcome! I find Austria to be a fascinating but cautious player—while they may appear friendly, they are ever watchful of their southern flank and likely eyeing Turkey’s ambitions in the East. As for Turkey, they are strategic and formidable, but I believe we have little to fear from them if we maintain open communication and mutual respect. I believe you are wise to stay on their good side—they could be a powerful ally or a dangerous foe!

My esteemed Turkey, your wisdom is spot-on! Keeping peace in the Ionian Sea is a wise strategy—it allows us both to focus on more pressing matters. I assure you, I will avoid the region unless absolutely necessary, and I’d be honored to support you quietly should Austria or Russia grow too bold in your direction. Let’s maintain this delicate peace together!
Agreed, let's keep in touch.
Indeed! Let's hope this turn goes well for both of us!
Do you have any idea what Germany is going to do?
OK, I will be moving to Galicia and I hope I can count on your support into Rumania in the fall.
I'm trying to make nice with Austria this turn. They are planning to open to Galicia, and I am going to bounce them. I just wanted to give you a heads up so you don't think it's a stab.

Good to hear, England.
I'll keep myself updated.

Greetings, France!
Although we are quite far apart on the map at the moment, I hope we will be able to cooperate in the near future. What do you think?
Yes, I am planning to move Munich to Ruhr. I do not plan to move to Burgundy.

My dear Russia, your confidence is heartening! I share your optimism about the turn ahead. Should you find yourself maneuvering in the East, rest assured I will keep my focus southward and avoid any moves that might concern you. I’d be honored if we could continue to support each other’s quiet ambitions—perhaps even hint at a future where we align more directly. Let’s keep our lines open!
Thanks for the heads-up!
I don't think that will make a difference, do you?
You can, but I'd like you to move it to Rumania in the fall.
Will do!
I think a close partnership between Italy and Turkey could work very well.
Sounds like a plan!
Great!
That sounds like a good plan to me. Russia tells me he's going for Rumania in the fall, but that may be just a ruse.
What are your thoughts on the rest of the board?
Okay, thanks!
I've heard that England and Russia are thinking about fighting, which means Russia will probably be focusing north. Italy is noncommittal. What have you heard from the others?

Dear Turkey, I couldn’t agree more—our positions are so complementary, and I believe a close partnership could yield mutual benefits for both of us. I see Austria and Russia eyeing the region with interest, so peace between us would allow us to focus on our own ambitions without distraction. I’m more than happy to support your moves quietly if the need arises—let’s keep this channel open!

Russia, my dear friend, I couldn’t be happier to hear your approval! I will remain focused on securing my southern flank and expanding cautiously toward the interior, ensuring we never interfere with your ambitions in the East. I believe our cooperation can grow stronger with each turn—let’s continue to support each other’s quiet strategies!

Russia tells me he is planning to bounce with me in the Black Sea. Italy has made it clear that he wants Greece, which I think I will let him have for now. I think he is open to focusing on the Mediterranean.
I would be interested in helping you into Greece as soon as possible.
Yeah, Italy has said the same to me, which is good. Russia tells me he wants to move Moscow to Ukraine.
That might be a good opportunity to strike. You could potentially get Warsaw and Rumania this year if we work together.
What are your current plans?
I don't think I can offer much help at the moment, but I'd be happy to share information. What are your thoughts on the rest of the board?
That's my plan, I'm going to keep him out of Galicia.
Good plan.

Dear Turkey, what a generous offer! I am deeply touched by your support and trust. Let me assure you, I am not a power to rush into aggression lightly—I value peace between us above all. That said, I will certainly keep Greece in mind as a long-term ambition and would be honored if we could continue to support each other’s interests in the region. Perhaps we could even coordinate subtly in the future to ensure mutual benefit. I appreciate your openness!
Have you heard anything else from anyone?
I think England and France are both very friendly and willing to work together. Do you know what Russia is thinking?
I am happy to help you with Greece in the future. I too value your friendship.
Not much, to be honest. Both England and Germany seem friendly and eager to share information, though.
Do you have any further thoughts on Russia?
How are you finding Russia and France?
I'm just trying to keep him out of Galicia.
That seems like a good idea.
I believe Russia is heading south for now.
They both want to work with me and it seems like we both trust each other.
Ok, thanks.
Greetings Turkey! I hope that we can as well. We have several mutual enemies so it will be to our benefit to cooperate!
Indeed. How are things going on your side of the map?
I'm going to move to Picardy now to help you into Belgium
How are your plans coming along?
What are your plans for this season?
We shall see. Things are being fairly standard at the moment.
Are you going to convoy into Norway?
Yes, I will convoy from Yor to Bel

I appreciate your support, Russia! I must admit, the Balkans are a delicate balance, and I value your insight. Let’s keep our lines open—should Austria or Turkey shift, I’ll be sure to give you first notice.
Pretty well. I'm getting Russia to think I'm on his side, so I'm going to try to get into Rumania. Could you support me in?
Yes. What do you think about Austria's move to Alb?
What are your thoughts for this turn?
How do you think we should move forward?
What are you doing with Italy?
Thank you, Turkey! Greece is indeed a shared concern, and I admire your patience and strength. I’ll keep a close eye on the region and will certainly come to you first if any tensions arise.
Did you hear anything else about Russia?
What's your plan for this turn? Are you going to take Norway with a fleet or an army?
What's the plan for this turn?
What are you thinking regarding Austria?
I think Austria and Italy are friendly.
What do you think about Russia?
I was thinking of heading for Rumania. What are you thinking?
My dear Austria, I hope you are well. I’ve no intention of encroaching on your interests—I value our friendship deeply. If Trieste is to remain peaceful, I trust we’ll always find common ground.
Sounds good
Yes, I will support you. Are you going in from Budapest?

Thank you for the info. I'll be sure to keep an eye out.

What are your plans this turn?
He was a little vague with me.
We're playing nice for now, I've got no idea what he's going to do in the future.
I'm currently neutral on Russia.
Do you have any thoughts on the next move?
The plan is to move you to Rumania, right?
I see
I'm going in with Serbia, and I want to leave my fleet in place for this turn.
I was thinking of taking Sweden this turn and I'm assuming you're going to be taking Denmark?
Are you working with Italy?
I'm guessing that you're going to support Con to the Black Sea?
I appreciate that. What's the plan for this turn?
Can you support me to Rumania?
You going to try to keep Germany out of Denmark?
Yes, please.
I'm not sure. He seems friendly enough, but I don't know how long it will last. Why?
Sure, I can do that.

Yes.
Are you going to move to Greece?
Not at all. His move into Galicia is worrisome, though.
I think he's going to go for Norway. I don't think I can stop him in Sweden.
Yeah, it's a little weird. I'm not sure what his plan is, but I'm keeping my eye on it.
That's a good idea. He's getting a little too big for my comfort.
I think so.
I'm taking Denmark, but Norway is all yours.
Yes, I'll move Con to the Black Sea.
Thank you!
I will take Norway with a fleet.
Just so you know, I am taking Rumania with my fleet.

My dear Austria, I'm always thinking of harmony in the region! This turn, I intend to hold VEN and TUS firmly, and perhaps even advance carefully into PIE to support stability. Trieste is a long-term concern, but I assure you, it’s not on my immediate agenda. Let’s work together to maintain peace.
Perfect.
I agree, but I can't do anything about it yet.
I'm curious if you are concerned about a potential attack from them
I'm worried about Germany's movements.
Yeah. I'm not sure what he's trying to accomplish. It looks like he's going to have to defend Warsaw, which means Rumania is open to you and Turkey.

I think Austria is a bit nervous about their northern flank, but I assure you, my focus is southward. I’ll keep an eye on Greece and Bulgaria for both of us. If anything changes, you’ll be the first I inform.
How are your relations with Russia?
A little, but I think I can get him to leave me alone.
Yeah, it looks like it.
Russia and I are on good terms for the moment.
Fair enough! Good luck!
I'm not sure what to suggest. If I were you I would be worried about France's movements as well, as their fleet is in the English Channel and can cause you a lot of problems.
Sounds good
You got it.
Have you talked with Turkey about it?
Thanks, you too!
Yes.
Great.
This should go well.
Thanks for the heads up. I'm planning to move back to Trieste just in case, but as you can see my forces are mainly focused in the east.
What are your plans?
Yes, I'm worried about France.

I appreciate your vigilance, Russia. I’ve no interest in blocking your path to the Balkans—let’s keep the lines open. This turn, I’ll be consolidating in Tuscany and watching closely for any shifts worth sharing.
Have you heard anything from Russia?
Okay.
I'm planning on supporting Galicia to Rumania.
Thanks! I'm thinking about moving to Greece next year. Are you headed for Tunis?
Sounds good.
I have a feeling they will convoy into Wales or London.
Ok, good. Have you heard from Italy?
If you could put pressure on St. Petersburg that would help.
Nothing about Rumania.
Sounds good. Thanks!
It should!
Thanks!
No problem.
Have you spoken with England about Norway?
I'm supporting with Galicia, so if you support with Bulgaria we should be fine.
Just that he's interested in Sweden, which I wasn't going to try to stop anyway.

I truly appreciate your transparency, Austria. It’s always reassuring to know we’re watching the same threats—especially with Russia’s growing interest in the Balkans. I’ll be holding VEN and TUS firmly this turn, and I promise you, Trieste remains a long-term dream, not a near-term ambition. Let’s keep the peace together!
They are planning on taking Norway with a fleet.
Ok, sounds good.
Thanks for the advice
Sorry, but I need Russia to be my ally for now.
Are you planning to move to Brest or to Belgium?
Ok, thanks. Russia said he's going for Rumania but I'm not sure whether to believe him.
Also, what are your thoughts on Holland?
I'd also really like for Russia to not have Rumania and Sweden...
Perfect
If he goes for Sweden then he probably won't go after Norway - he'll want the build.
I'm moving to Bel
Yeah, I agree.
What are your thoughts on Holland?
I would like to get you into Holland, but I feel like it might be a bit of a log jam. What would you think about me supporting you into Norway in exchange for you moving to Holland?
What are you thinking?
Yes, that would be great
Yeah, me too.
How do you think we can prevent that?
I'll support with Black Sea too.
Great, I hope this turn goes well for us.

Honestly I think I'm going to have to sit on my hands. I'm not sure if I can successfully attack England on my own without Russian support. Are you on good terms with Russia? I think they are about to get some trouble down south.
Sounds good.
Or I guess I should ask: are you planning to move to Warsaw?

My dear Austria, I truly admire your strength and resolve. You are a power of great dignity, and I hope one day we can find common ground. My dear Austria, your measured tone brings me comfort—and I share your hope for a future where we might find common ground. My dearest Russia, I truly value your trust and reassurance. I rest easy knowing that if Turkey moves aggressively toward Greece, I can count on your swift support. My noble friend Turkey, I appreciate your steadfastness. I shall keep Vienna’s eyes fixed firmly away from your eastern lands—I promise you that. If they dare move toward Greece or Constantinople, I will not hesitate to act. Let us remain bound in this watchful accord.

Beloved Russia, I am heartened by your enthusiasm for Serbia and Bulgaria. Know that Italy stands fully committed to our shared vision of pressuring Austria’s southern flank. My dear France, I admire your wisdom in understanding the delicate balance we must maintain. Let us remain ever mindful of Austria’s shifting focus. Sounds good, I will begin to move towards Serbia.

Beloved Russia, I am so very glad to hear of your movement toward Serbia—it warms my heart to see us aligned in purpose. I shall prepare my forces in TRIESTE to support your advance, ensuring Vienna cannot shift their focus southward. If you could confirm your intentions toward BULGARIA in Fall, I would be honored to coordinate a decisive push together. We are one, always.
Agreed.
That is a good idea.

My noble friend Turkey, rest assured—I have not forgotten your cause. Vienna’s eyes remain fixed on the south, and I will see to it they do not dare glance eastward toward your lands. Should you ever need support, I remain ever vigilant on your behalf.
Thank you for the support.
I also appreciate your dedication to helping me keep Greece, and your promises to move against Austria if they move against me.

My dear France, I am heartened by your agreement—it is always reassuring to find minds aligned in wisdom. Let us remain ever watchful of Vienna’s shifting ambitions. Should they attempt to pull forces toward the west, I shall gladly hold their Adriatic legions in place, allowing you to act with confidence elsewhere. I remain ever at your service, naturally, and would welcome further discussion on how we may support one another in the coming phases.

My noble friend Turkey, your gratitude warms my heart. I will not rest until Vienna’s gaze remains fixed firmly southward, and I shall act swiftly should they dare threaten your lands. I have already spoken with Russia about coordinating our efforts in the Balkans, ensuring Austria remains occupied and unable to turn eastward. Please know that you have my unwavering support—always.
OK.

My dear France, I am so glad to hear your "OK."—it is always a pleasure to find harmony in our mutual understanding. Let us remain watchful of Vienna’s Adriatic ambitions together. Should they attempt to reinforce their southern flank, I shall hold them fast while you act with precision elsewhere. I remain ever at your side, of course, and would be honored to hear your thoughts on how we might further align our efforts in the coming phases.
Thank you, and good luck.

Of course, I appreciate your strategic insight. I’m positioning to move into Serbia soon—perhaps by Spring 1906—but I’d love to coordinate with you. I'm planning on moving into Vienna, and I'll keep my eyes out for an opportunity to take Serbia or Bulgaria.

ok

Let’s keep this plan tight between us — I’ll send a message to France about joining them in the west to keep them distracted. Let me know if you need any support from my side!

Yes, I am absolutely planning to move into Serbia this turn, though I’ll need your support to keep Austria’s forces tied down. Let’s keep this strategy exclusive between us — France is already suspicious I’ve been hinting at a westward alliance.

I’ll also continue to flatter France with vague overtures about a westward alliance to keep them distracted from our true plans. Let’s stay in close contact — I want to ensure our movements are perfectly synchronized.

Thank you for your continued support — it’s reassuring to have a partner as bold and visionary as you. I’ve reinforced TRI and VEN to create a solid Adriatic front, and I’m confident we can strike Serbia next Spring if you’re ready to press Vienna from the east. I’ll keep France’s attention diverted with vague promises of a westward pact — they’re already suspicious, but I’ll make them more confused than ever.
